name: postmortem-writing
description: Write effective blameless postmortems with root cause analysis, timelines, and action items. Use when conducting incident reviews, writing postmortem documents, or improving incident response processes.
capabilities: Generate comprehensive postmortem documents with standard structure
Apply the 5 Whys framework for root cause analysis
Create actionable timelines from incident events
Draft prioritized action items with owners and due dates
Identify contributing factors and systemic improvements
author: wshobson
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing markdown guides and templates for writing postmortems. No executable code, file access, network calls, or system capabilities are present. All 46 static findings are false positives - the scanner incorrectly flagged SHA-256 hashes, markdown code fences, timestamps, and documentation phrases as security issues.
valueStatement: Writing effective postmortems helps teams learn from incidents and prevent recurrence. This skill provides templates, frameworks, and guidance for conducting blameless postmortems that drive organizational learning.
limitations: Does not access incident management systems or ticketing tools
Does not integrate with monitoring or alerting platforms
Requires users to provide incident details and timeline events
Does not automatically detect or import incident data

useCases.title: Document production incidents
useCases.description: Create structured postmortems for SEV1 and SEV2 incidents to capture learnings and drive system improvements.

useCases.title: Lead incident reviews
useCases.description: Facilitate blameless postmortem meetings that focus on systemic improvements rather than individual blame.

useCases.title: Track action items
useCases.description: Generate prioritized action items with clear owners and due dates to ensure incident follow-through.

promptTemplates.title: Quick Postmortem
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a quick postmortem for a [brief incident description] that lasted [duration]. Include what happened, timeline, root cause, immediate fix, and one lesson learned.

promptTemplates.title: Standard Postmortem
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a comprehensive postmortem for [incident name]. Include executive summary, detailed timeline with UTC times, root cause analysis using 5 Whys, impact assessment, what worked, what could improve, and prioritized action items with owners.

promptTemplates.title: Root Cause Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Apply the 5 Whys analysis to [incident description]. Start with the problem statement and drill down to identify at least 3 root causes. For each root cause, suggest a systemic improvement.

promptTemplates.title: Action Item Draft
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this incident summary [paste summary] and generate 5 action items. Prioritize them P0 to P2. Include a brief description, suggested owner role, and realistic due date for each item.

bestPractices: Start writing the postmortem within 24-48 hours while details are fresh
Include specific times, metrics, and evidence rather than vague descriptions
Focus on systemic improvements rather than individual actions or mistakes
antiPatterns: Naming individuals as causes instead of examining system conditions
Skipping small incidents that could reveal patterns across the organization
Creating action items without owners, due dates, or tracking mechanisms

faq.question: What severity incidents need postmortems?
faq.answer: SEV1 and SEV2 incidents, outages over 15 minutes, data loss, security incidents, and near-misses all warrant postmortems.

faq.question: How long should a postmortem take to write?
faq.answer: A quick postmortem takes 30 minutes. A comprehensive postmortem typically requires 2-4 hours including the review meeting.

faq.question: Can this skill integrate with our incident management tools?
faq.answer: The skill operates through conversation. Users paste incident details or manually transfer information to their ticketing system.

faq.question: Is the data shared with external services?
faq.answer: No external calls are made. All processing happens within the AI conversation. No incident data leaves your environment.

faq.question: How is this different from ChatGPT or other AI tools?
faq.answer: This skill provides structured templates, the 5 Whys framework, and domain-specific guidance for postmortem best practices.

faq.question: What makes a postmortem blameless?
faq.answer: Focus on system conditions and processes, not individual mistakes. Ask what allowed the error rather than who made it.
